description: Multi-scale RAR analysis: Amram cluster galaxy rotation curves + published cluster-scale RAR results (Tian+2020, Chan & Del Popolo)

level1_amram.limitation: No baryonic decomposition — cannot compute g_bar or construct full RAR

interpretations: Baryonic mass systematics at cluster scale

verdict: INCONCLUSIVE but informative. Amram cluster galaxy data lacks baryonic decomposition for direct RAR construction. Published cluster-scale RAR shows a different acceleration scale (~17× g†), which either indicates mass-dependent condensation or distinct cluster physics. This is an open question that does NOT undermine the galaxy-scale result but does constrain the theory. The Letter should note this as a testable prediction: if g† is truly universal, improved cluster baryonic mass measurements should bring a0_cluster closer to g†.
